summary: Large item-system matching PR touching 28 files. It converted several item object files from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, made extensive item C/source cleanup and header/type updates, and adjusted symbol metadata for local/global data, sdata, and sdata2 objects. The bot report records +0.31% matched code, 43 new matches, 30 unmatched-item improvements, and one small unmatched .data regression in main/melee/it/it_2725.

key_files.role: Marked item objects as Matching, including itsamuschargeshot, itgamewatchchef, itkamex, itunknown, ithouou, and itwhitebea.
Adjusted item-area data/sdata/sdata2 symbol scope, local labels, and sizes, including multiple global address-style symbols converted to local @ labels.
Largest source matching target; bot report lists many new matches and improvements in main/melee/it/it_2725, plus one small .data regression.

what_changed: Converted six item source objects in configure.py from NonMatching to Matching: itsamuschargeshot, itgamewatchchef, itkamex, itunknown, ithouou, and itwhitebea.

Performed large source matching edits in item TUs, especially it_2725.c, itcoll.c, itlinkboomerang.c, itsamuschargeshot.c, itwhitebea.c, itunknown.c, itsscope.c, ithouou.c, itkamex.c, and itgamewatchchef.c.

Refined item and fighter-facing types, including replacing HSD_ObjAllocUnk2 with Item_FtTrack, adding x150_count, adding itHouou_ItemVars, and changing item attribute fields from padding or generic scalar types into typed fields, arrays, pointers, and unions.
Adjusted config/GALE01/symbols.txt local/global symbol metadata for item-related .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 entries, including many address-style global float/string symbols becoming local @ labels.
The decomp-dev bot reported 43 new matches, 30 improvements in unmatched items, +12020 matched-code bytes, +20644 linked-code bytes, and one small .data regression in main/melee/it/it_2725.
smart_moves: The PR updated configure.py only for item objects whose status the bot report supports as newly matching or complete enough for the PR goal.
Header/type work accompanied source changes instead of leaving all recovered knowledge as local casts or unknown object types.
Symbol metadata was adjusted in the same PR as item data/text matching, making local label and scope changes visible for review rather than hidden in source-only edits.
The bot report quantified both wins and the one .data regression, giving reviewers a compact ledger of text/data impact.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When item matching reveals a cross-subsystem structure, update the owning forward declarations and prototypes together; this PR changed HSD_ObjAllocUnk2 to Item_FtTrack in forward.h, ftCo_DownAttack.c, and ftCo_DownAttack.h.

Typed item variables and attributes can replace padding, UNK_T, and overly generic scalar fields during matching; examples include itHouou_ItemVars, itWhiteBea_ItemVars.x20 as Item_GObj*, and itUnknown union fields.

Source matching may require symbol metadata review for data/sdata/sdata2 ownership, but the evidence should distinguish code progress from data regressions; this PR had broad code gains and one reported it_2725 .data regression.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot report regression in main/melee/it/it_2725 .data

naming_conventions.name: Item_FtTrack
naming_conventions.convention: Recovered semantic type name replacing HSD_ObjAllocUnk2 for a fighter/item tracking structure.

naming_conventions.name: x150_count
naming_conventions.convention: Field name retained offset-style prefix while adding a supported role suffix for a count field.

naming_conventions.name: itHouou_ItemVars, itKamexAttributes, itWhiteBea_ItemVars, itUnknownAttributes
naming_conventions.convention: Item-specific structs follow existing item-name plus role suffix style for item variables and attributes.

naming_conventions.name: @NNN local labels
naming_conventions.convention: symbols.txt uses local @ labels for some local string/float/double data entries instead of global address-style it_ symbols when metadata is changed to local scope.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Promote objects from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py only alongside reported object/function match evidence.
Use recovered struct fields, arrays, unions, and pointer types to drive codegen rather than keeping raw padding or opaque object alloc types.
Several source hunks used matching steering constructs such as PAD_STACK, #pragma dont_inline push/pop blocks, GET_JOBJ cleanup, and ABS macro expressions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The slice shows tactic use and a successful aggregate bot report, but not per-tactic objdiff justification for each instance.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjust symbol scope, label, and size metadata for data sections when text/data matching evidence points at local literals or data ownership changes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: One .data regression was reported, so broad symbol edits should remain review-scoped rather than treated as universally safe.

notes: PR body is empty and there are no review comments or reviews in the supplied slice, so reviewer intent and author rationale are not available.
The raw diff is truncated in loaded_files but the changed-file list, key diff excerpts, and bot report are available.
Bot report provides aggregate verification-style evidence for matches and regressions, but the slice does not include per-function objdiff commands or per-tactic validation notes.
Source and symbol metadata changes are evident, but the intake does not independently validate whether each data scope change was required.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Cross-subsystem type recovery should update all visible signatures together
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2432 replaced HSD_ObjAllocUnk2 with Item_FtTrack across forward.h, ftCo_DownAttack.c, and ftCo_DownAttack.h, preserving consistency between recovered source type and public prototype.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header field recovery can unlock item matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2432 refined item headers by replacing padding, UNK_T, and generic scalars with typed arrays, item pointers, animation pointers, and unions in item vars/attributes while advancing many item matches.

source_update_candidates.title: configure.py MatchingFor controls item object Matching/NonMatching status
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2432 changed six item object entries in configure.py from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...) for item TUs after source matching work.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact about where Matching/NonMatching status is declared; curator should verify against current build configuration.

source_update_candidates.title: Record per-tactic justification when PAD_STACK or pragmas remain in item matching code
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2432 contains PAD_STACK and #pragma dont_inline usage in item matching source, while the loaded slice only provides aggregate bot report evidence. A source-corpus note could remind contributors to pair such tactics with narrow objdiff/build evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is already broadly covered by loaded standards, but this PR is a concrete historical example candidate rather than a new accepted rule.

source_update_candidates.title: Review item data/sdata/sdata2 local label ownership changes from PR 2432
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2432 changed many item-area symbols from global address-style names to local @ labels and adjusted one .data object size/local label, while the bot report recorded broad data gains and one small it_2725 .data regression.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential data ownership/symbol metadata example; curator should not promote all local-label changes as generally correct without current symbol/source verification.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all item TUs became fully matched; the PR title says >90%, configure.py promotes six objects, and the bot report still lists unmatched-item improvements and a .data regression.
Do not claim reviewer approval details beyond merge status; the slice has zero review comments and zero reviews.
Do not treat every PAD_STACK or #pragma use in this PR as accepted style; the slice lacks per-instance validation evidence.
Do not generalize the symbols.txt global-to-local conversions into a blanket rule for item data; they are PR-specific metadata edits with one reported data regression.
